title: Studies on the Stability Constants of Vanadium-N-Cinnamoyl-N-Phenyl Hydroxylamine Complexes-A 1 : 2 Metal-Ligand System
author: SASWATI P. BAG
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur University, Calcutta-700032 

  Manuscript received 12 March 1973; revised 12 November 1973; accepted 18 February 1974  

 The stability constants of a 1 : 2 metal-ligand complex system have been evaluated spectra-photometrically by graphical extrapolation methods following Yatsimirskii's and Leden's ideas for calculation of K 1  and K 2 . For the present study we have selected N-Cinnamoyl-N-phenyl hydro.. xylamine as the most sensitive reagent among the arylhydroxylamine derivatives. The log K 1  and log K 2  of the vanadium complexes are found to be 4.62 and 4.41 by Yatsimirskii's method and those by Leden's method are 4.81 and 4.74 respectively.
